mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, labor market deterioration triggers recession expectations that compress corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses 6.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation signals demand-pull inflationary pressure that forces monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL and RSAFS both rise, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during recovery regimes, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance with t-statistic below 1.5 when INDPRO increases.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising consumer spending capacity, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently coincides with negative 20-day forward returns, or the correlation coefficient falls below 0.05 across all tested sub-periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index increase exceeding 0.5% triggers immediate market repricing of future interest rate paths, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 70% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.5%, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for prior rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in housing starts and real GDP growth confirms broad-based domestic demand strength, reducing recession probability and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns when both series expand, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in industrial production below its six-month moving average signals deteriorating manufacturing momentum that triggers short-term risk-off positioning, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when INDPRO crosses below its moving average, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during late-cycle phases.
